questions.q: Dr. Zakir Husain served as which President of India?
questions.explanation: Dr. Zakir Husain was the third President of India.

questions.q: Dr. Zakir Husain holds the distinction of being the first holder of which background to become President of India?
questions.explanation: He was the first Muslim to hold the office of President of India.

questions.q: In which year did Dr. Zakir Husain become President of India?
questions.explanation: He became the third President of India in 1967.

questions.q: What notable event ended Dr. Zakir Husain's presidency in 1969?
questions.explanation: He died in office in 1969, making him the first President to do so.

questions.q: For his work in which field is Dr. Zakir Husain especially remembered?
questions.explanation: He was an eminent educationist, earning the title 'the Educator President'.

questions.q: With which educational institution is Dr. Zakir Husain closely associated?
questions.explanation: He was closely associated with Jamia Millia Islamia as one of its founders and leaders.

questions.q: What academic title is commonly used with Zakir Husain's name?
questions.explanation: He is commonly referred to as Dr. Zakir Husain.

questions.q: Whom did Dr. Zakir Husain succeed as President of India?
questions.explanation: He succeeded Dr. S. Radhakrishnan as the third President in 1967.

questions.q: Dr. Zakir Husain served as the constitutional head of which country?
questions.explanation: He served as the President of the Republic of India.

questions.q: Dr. Zakir Husain is popularly described by which epithet reflecting his career?
questions.explanation: He is remembered as the Educator President for his contributions to education.

questions.q: Which office did Dr. Zakir Husain hold immediately before becoming President of India?
questions.explanation: He was Vice-President of India from 1962 to 1967, before becoming President.

questions.q: Dr. Zakir Husain served as Governor of which state before becoming Vice-President?
questions.explanation: He served as Governor of Bihar from 1957 to 1962.

questions.q: Who succeeded Dr. Zakir Husain following his death, initially as Acting President in 1969?
questions.explanation: V.V. Giri, then Vice-President, became Acting President after Zakir Husain's death.

questions.q: Dr. Zakir Husain was awarded India's highest civilian honour. Which one?
questions.explanation: He was awarded the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian honour.

questions.q: Dr. Zakir Husain served as Vice-Chancellor of Jamia Millia Islamia and later of which other major university?
questions.explanation: He served as Vice-Chancellor of Aligarh Muslim University in the 1940s.

questions.q: Dr. Zakir Husain was the first President of India to do which of the following?
questions.explanation: He was the first Indian President to die while still in office, in 1969.

questions.q: During which year did Dr. Zakir Husain pass away while serving as President?
questions.explanation: He died in office in May 1969.

questions.q: As Vice-President, Dr. Zakir Husain served as ex-officio Chairman of which House of Parliament?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: Which educational philosophy movement of the 1930s, focused on craft-based learning, was Dr. Zakir Husain associated with?
questions.explanation: He helped develop the Basic Education or Nai Talim scheme associated with Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.q: In which year did Dr. Zakir Husain become Vice-President of India?
questions.explanation: He was elected Vice-President in 1962, succeeding S. Radhakrishnan in that office.

questions.q: Dr. Zakir Husain's tenure as President fell during the term of which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: His presidency from 1967 to 1969 coincided with Indira Gandhi's tenure as Prime Minister.

questions.q: The Vice-President's office that Dr. Zakir Husain held is the second-highest constitutional office in which country?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is the second-highest constitutional office in India.

questions.q: In which year was Dr. Zakir Husain born?
questions.explanation: He was born on 8 February 1897.

questions.q: Jamia Millia Islamia, the institution Dr. Zakir Husain helped lead, was originally founded in which city before moving to Delhi?
questions.explanation: Jamia Millia Islamia was founded at Aligarh in 1920 before relocating to Delhi.

questions.q: Dr. Zakir Husain pursued doctoral studies in economics in which country?
questions.explanation: He earned a doctorate in economics from a university in Germany.

questions.q: Dr. Zakir Husain chaired a committee in the late 1930s that produced a noted report on which subject?
questions.explanation: He chaired the committee that produced the report on the scheme of Basic National Education.

questions.q: Before the Bharat Ratna, Dr. Zakir Husain had earlier received which of these civilian honours?
questions.explanation: He received the Padma Vibhushan before being awarded the Bharat Ratna.

questions.q: Dr. Zakir Husain was nominated as a member of which House of Parliament before holding higher constitutional office?
questions.explanation: He was a nominated member of the Rajya Sabha in the 1950s.

questions.q: Following Dr. Zakir Husain's death in office, a presidential election was held in which year to choose his successor?
questions.explanation: A presidential election was held in 1969 after his death, won by V.V. Giri.

questions.q: Dr. Zakir Husain's term as Vice-President ended in which year, when he became President?
questions.explanation: His Vice-Presidential term ran from 1962 to 1967, after which he became President.
